content.problemStatement:
You are given a directed graph with nnn vertices and mmm edges. A walk is defined as a sequence of vertices, such that there is an edge from every vertex in the walk to the next vertex in the walk.

Formally, a walk with ttt edges is a sequence v1,v2,…vt+1v_1, v_2, \ldots v_{t+1}v1​,v2​,…vt+1​, where for each 1≤i≤t1 \leq i \leq t1≤i≤t, there is an edge from viv_ivi​ to vi+1v_{i + 1}vi+1​.

The vertices viv_ivi​ of a walk do not have to be distinct. A single edge can occur multiple times in a walk.

Call a walk beautiful if it has atmost ⌊n24⌋\left \lfloor \frac{n^2}{4}  \right \rfloor⌊4n2​⌋ edges and each vertex is present in it at least once.

There are multiple independent testcases. For each testcase, find a beautiful walk in the given graph or claim that it doesn't exist.
constraints: The sum of nnn over all testcases doesn't exceed 100010001000.
There are no multiple edges in the input. Note that it is allowed to have an edge from iii to jjj and another from jjj to iii though.

`ExplanationIn the first testcase, the walk 1→2→3→1→4→51 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow1 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 51→2→3→1→4→5 is a beautiful walk, as it visits all vertices and has 5≤5245 \leq \frac{5^2}{4}5≤452​ edges.
Note that `5 1 4 5 1 2 3` is also a valid output.
In the second test case, there is no beautiful walk, so you sould print −1-1−1.
content.inputFormat:
First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains of nnn and mmm, the number of vertices and the number of edges in the graph respectively.
The next mmm lines describe the edges of the graph. Each line consists of two integers aaa and bbb denoting a directed edge from aaa to bbb.
content.outputFormat:
For each testcase:


If there doesn't exist a beautiful walk, print −1-1−1 on a newline.
If there exists a beautiful walk, find any such walk. Say it has l≤n24l \leq \frac{n^2}{4}l≤4n2​ edges, and the sequence of vertices is {v1,v2,…vl+1}\{v_1, v_2, \ldots v_{l + 1} \}{v1​,v2​,…vl+1​}. Print lll followed by v1,v2,…vl+1v_1, v_2, \ldots v_{l + 1}v1​,v2​,…vl+1​ on a new line.
